content: Audit logging pitfalls for SaaS: (1) Conflating application logs with audit logs — different retention, immutability, and query requirements; (2) Async write to audit log table without synchronous capture means events can be silently lost on app crash; (3) PII in audit logs is a permanent liability — immutable rows with email addresses, IPs, user-agents create GDPR exposure; (4) No tamper evidence — Postgres rows can be deleted or updated; use append-only tables with Postgres row-level security (no UPDATE/DELETE privileges) and optionally hash chains; (5) Logging command content in remote terminal audit trail exposes secrets typed by user; (6) Missing tenant context on audit rows allows cross-tenant log leakage in multi-tenant queries; (7) Audit log write failures silently swallowed mean security-relevant events are never recorded.
